rollup.narrative: 8 active/deferred incidents remain in the current operating picture. The leading current classification is Rescue, with current concerns associated with Apas.

decision_points.body: 8 critical/high-priority reports; 2 injury/patient signals; 1 rescue/stranded signal remain in the current picture; leadership may need to prioritize rescue, EMS, welfare, shelter, and protection coordination before ordinary logistics.

decision_points.body: Current requested demand totals 58 units across active/deferred reports. Leadership may need to consider whether existing response capacity is enough for open life-safety, welfare, public safety, infrastructure, and water-support needs.

rollup.executive_assessment: 8 active/deferred incident reports remain open across Apas. Life-safety signals include 8 critical/high-priority reports; 2 injury/patient signals; 1 rescue/stranded signal. No blocked or limited access report is present. Current pressure is driven by Rescue, Infrastructure Damage, Building/House Fire, Public Disturbance / Riot, with 8 in-progress assignments and 58 current requested resource units associated with open reports.

rollup.intro: This section separates operational constraints from information limits so leaders can judge what might block a clean decision.

items.title: Resource supply not confirmed
items.evidence: 12 requested resource rows remain tied to active/deferred incidents. Resource evidence uses canonical Hotline resource_types records.
items.confidence_note: Hotline records the request; dispatch, arrival, delivery, consumption, and sufficiency are not confirmed by this SITREP.
items.decision_relevance: Leadership can see current demand, but should not treat the requested units as confirmed supply or completed delivery.

items.title: Road/access constraints may affect field movement
items.evidence: 3 current route constraints reported: 2 blocked, 1 limited.
items.confidence_note: Road/access conditions are reported by incident records and should be verified before public routing guidance or major deployment assumptions.
items.decision_relevance: Route constraints can affect response timing, staging decisions, and whether public routing advisories should be issued.

items.title: Population figures require verification
items.evidence: 6 current population/life-safety records reported: People injured, Patient or injured person, Estimated people involved, Evacuation Needed.
items.confidence_note: Family, shelter, evacuation, patient, missing-person, and affected-person fields may overlap.
items.decision_relevance: Population fields should guide life-safety awareness, but should not be used as one consolidated affected-person total without validation.

items.title: Closed and discarded reports are not current pressure
items.evidence: 5 resolved reports were treated as addressed history; 2 discarded reports were excluded from posture, demand, and severity.
items.confidence_note: A resolved report cannot carry pending entries under current Hotline rules; discarded reports are neglected for current posture.
items.decision_relevance: This keeps the operating picture focused on reports that still need leadership visibility or downstream coordination.
